params.name: Ramsar Convention
params.description:
On Nov 10, 1984, the Ramsar Convention added new protected areas.
The Ramsar Convention is the world's first modern international environmental treaty. The convention establishes a basic framework for the conservation and sustainable use of wetland resources, while also encouraging contracting parties to designate Ramsar wetlands within their own territories, which are wetland areas of special environmental importance, biodiversity, or international significance.
